commit_message:
ESQL: Fix limit task test (#117270)

Fix a test for the task results when running the `LIMIT` operation. We
were releasing a few permits to get the query started. And when you
combine that with the page worth of permits that the test was releasing
we'd sometimes finish the entire limited query, stopping the task too
early to find a running task.

Closes #107293

issue_title: Simulate pipeline API not working with ignore_missing_pipeline
commit_message:
Simulate should succeed if ignore_missing_pipeline (#108106)

PipelineProcessors with non-existing pipelines should succeed (as noop)
 if ignore_missing_pipeline=true. Currently, does not work when pipelines are
 simulated with verbose=true. In this case, an error is returned and no results
 are shown for subsequent processors. This change allows following processors
 to run, and changes the status from error to error_ignored.

issue_title: Requests to Elasticsearch with two `traceparent` headers should yield `400 Bad Request`
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

8.12.2

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

Darwin (though this happens on ESS, and an Elasticsearch docker image)

### Problem Description

Requests to Elasticsearch with two `traceparent` headers fail. I don't think they should, but I'm happy to be proven wrong.

We received an issue from a user that requests to Elasticsearch from the JS client were failing when their Node.js app was instrumented with the Elastic APM Node.js agent. It turns out that the user was using *two separate APM agents* on the app. With both APM agents running, HTTP requests to Elasticsearch would have *two* `traceparent` headers.

This section of the W3C trace-context spec (https://w3c.github.io/trace-context/#a-traceparent-is-received) says:

> 5.1 Processing Model for Working with Trace Context Request Header
> ...
> If a traceparent header is received:
>
>    1. The vendor checks an incoming request for a traceparent and a tracestate header.
>    2. Because the traceparent header is present, the vendor tries to parse the version of the traceparent header.
>        1. If the version cannot be parsed, the vendor creates a new traceparent header and deletes tracestate.
> ...

My read of that spec is that an invalid traceparent is received (I'd classify multiple traceparent headers as invalid, though the spec doesn't specifically say that), then the `traceparent` and `tracestate` headers should be ignored.



### Steps to Reproduce

```
curl -v $ES_URL -H traceparent:asdf -H traceparent:qwerty
```

For example:

```
% curl -v https://[REDACTED].es.us-west2.gcp.elastic-cloud.com/ -H traceparent:asdf -H traceparent:qwerty
...
> GET / HTTP/2
> Host:[REDACTED].es.us-west2.gcp.elastic-cloud.com
> User-Agent: curl/8.4.0
> Accept: */*
> traceparent:asdf
> traceparent:qwerty
>
* received GOAWAY, error=0, last_stream=1
< HTTP/2 502
< content-type: application/json; charset=UTF-8
< x-cloud-request-id: xHAxKfEoQKiYGTbKqHzfzw
< x-found-handling-cluster: [REDACTED]
< x-found-handling-instance: instance-0000000000
< content-length: 51
< date: Wed, 10 Apr 2024 21:58:48 GMT
<
{"ok":false,"message":"backend closed connection"}
```

A log message from a `v8.13.0-SNAPSHOT` test cluster of mine for a request list this:


commit_message:
Handle exceptions thrown by HTTP header validation (#107355)

Today if the HTTP header validation throws an exception (rather than
calling `listener.onFailure()`) then we treat this as a server-side
error, record it in the logs, and close the connection abruptly without
sending a response. In practice such an exception is more likely a
client-side error, so with this commit we catch it and marshal it back
to the client instead.

Closes #107338

issue_body:
### Description

In Elasticsearch index sorting does not work if nested fields are used. This Elasticsearch limitation derives from a Lucene limitation [which has been fixed recently](https://github.com/apache/lucene/pull/12829). We currently use index sorting for time series indices but plan to use it for logs too.

Note that at the moment nested fields are also not supported in synthetic source but we plan to address this limitation in #106460.

The limitation behind using index sorting and nested fields is a result of the fact that nested documents need to be written contiguously to their parent document inside the Lucene segment to make sure that the parent/child relationship is correctly mantained. Index sorting used to break this relationship in Lucene ,since sorting of parent documents was missing the logic to move nested document next to the linked parent document. This has been fixed recently so we need to make sure we make use of the Lucene functionality so that we can remove the limitation in Elasticsearch too. Failing to support nested fields while index sorting is used might make using the new "logs" index mode not possible, since `nested` fields are used by a few integrations indexing logs in logs-\*-\* data streams.

After reading the description of the Lucene fix it looks like the parent child relationship in Lucene is accomplished by using a (common) numeric value which is stored using numeric doc values. As a result, taking advantage of this feature in Lucene might require we change our implementation of nested fields to use numeric doc values too.
commit_message:
Support index sorting with nested fields (#110251)

This PR piggy-backs on recent changes in Lucene 9.11.1
(https://github.com/apache/lucene/pull/12829,
https://github.com/apache/lucene/pull/13341/), setting the parent doc
when nested fields are present. This allows moving nested documents
along with parent ones during sorting.

With this change, sorting is now allowed on fields outside nested
objects. Sorting on fields within nested objects is still not supported
(throws an exception).

Fixes #107349

We should probably have a more strict validation for these cases and return a more descriptive error message
commit_message:
Added stricter range type checks and runtime warnings for ENRICH (#115091)

It has been noted that strange or incorrect error messages are returned if the ENRICH command uses incompatible data types, for example a KEYWORD with value 'foo' using in an int_range match: https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/107357

This error is thrown at runtime and contradicts the ES|QL policy of only throwing errors at planning time, while at runtime we should instead set results to null and add a warning. However, we could make the planner stricter and block potentially mismatching types earlier.

However runtime parsing of KEYWORD fields has been a feature of ES|QL ENRICH since it's inception, in particular we even have tests asserting that KEYWORD fields containing parsable IP data can be joined to an ip_range ENRICH index.

In order to not create a backwards compatibility problem, we have compromised with the following:

* Strict range type checking at the planner time for incompatible range types, unless the incoming index field is KEYWORD
* For KEYWORD fields, allow runtime parsing of the fields, but when parsing fails, set the result to null and add a warning

Added extra tests to verify behaviour of match policies on non-keyword fields. They all behave as keywords (the enrich field is converted to keyword at policy execution time, and the input data is converted to keyword at lookup time).

issue_title: NoSuchRemoteClusterException is returned for both non-existent remotes and remotes with skip_unavailable=true
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version 8.14-SNAPSHOT ### Installed Plugins _No response_ ### Java Version _bundled_ ### OS Version Darwin michaels-mbp.lan 23.4.0 Darwin Kernel Version 23.4.0: Fri Mar 15 00:10:42 PDT 2024; root:xnu-10063.101.17~1/RELEASE_ARM64_T6000 arm64 ### Problem Description When doing cross-cluster searches (`_search` or `_async_search`), `NoSuchRemoteClusterException` is returned for both non-existent remotes and remotes with skip_unavailable=true that cannot be connected to. By contrast, remotes with skip_unavailable=false return a "connection_exception" error message when they cannot be reached. Should we also be returning "connection exception" errors for skip_unavailable=true remotes? Should NoSuchRemoteClusterException be reserved for attempts to search clusters that are not configured? This issue occurs for both non-secured and secured clusters. See the "Steps to Reproduce" section for details. ### Steps to Reproduce Manual testing. Configure a cluster to connect to two remote clusters. In my case, the three clusters all run on the same local machine and the config on the primary looks like: ``` cluster: remote: remote1: seeds: 127.0.0.1:9301 skip_unavailable: true remote2: seeds: 127.0.0.1:9302 skip_unavailable: false ```

commit_message:
NoSuchRemoteClusterException should not be thrown when a remote is configured (#107435)

When a remote cluster has been configured by an admin, but is not connected, clusters marked with skip_unavailable=true will have NoSuchRemoteClusterException thrown, which is confusing. This fix changes that for one of the connection lookup pathways.

Fixes #107381

commit_message:
ESQL: Make esql version required in REST requests (#107433)

* Enable corresponding validation in EsqlQueryRequest.
* Add the ESQL version to requests to /_query in integration tests.
* In mixed cluster tests for versions prior to 8.13.3, impersonate an 8.13
   client and do not send any version.

---------

Co-authored-by: Nik Everett <nik9000@gmail.com>

commit_message:
Fix SnapshotStatusApisIT#testInfiniteTimeout (#108294)

Make sure that the create-snapshot task completes on the master node before
we start checking the status of the snapshot

Resolve #107405

Co-authored-by: Elastic Machine <elasticmachine@users.noreply.github.com>

commit_message:
Address zstd release test failures in CodecTests. (#107477)

The tests can only be run when zstd feature flag is enabled.

Closes #107417

commit_message:
Avoid rolling over twice in searchable snapshot test (#108790)

Manually rolling over the data stream was 1. not necessary, and
2. causing issues when it happened after ILM had already rolled
over, which would make ILM convert the second backing index into a
searchable snapshot as well, possibly preventing the data stream
from being deleted.

commit_message:
Fix IndexShardTests#testFlushTimeExcludingWaiting (#108109)

It may take less than a millisecond to acquire/release a lock with no contention,
and flush stats have only millisecond resolution, so we can't guarantee that flush
time excluding waiting is actually less than flushTime after rounding.

So, we have to relax the assertion and make it `lessThanOrEqualTo` instead of
`lessThan`

Fixes #107462

commit_message:
Ensure executor is closed in case of failure (#107511)

This change ensures that executor is closed even in case of assertion failure
to prevent leaking threads.

commit_message:
Fail cluster state API if blocked (#109618)

It is important for callers of the cluster state API to be aware of any
blocks in place, particularly because the `STATE_NOT_RECOVERED_BLOCK`
means that the rest of the state is hidden from view. Yet if the caller
asks to suppress the `blocks` component of the response then they cannot
make this distinction. Thus, in this situation, we fail the request with
a block exception.

Fixes #107503

commit_message:
[ML] Wait for all shards to be active when creating the ML stats index (#108202)

* Wait for all shards to be active when creating the ML stats index

* Unmute tests

* Wait for the stats index in cleanup

* more waiting for the stats index

* Add adminclient to ensureHealth

Co-authored-by: Pat Whelan <pat.whelan@elastic.co>

* fix errors causing build failures

---------

Co-authored-by: Max Hniebergall <137079448+maxhniebergall@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Pat Whelan <pat.whelan@elastic.co>
Co-authored-by: Max Hniebergall <max.hniebergall@elastic.co>
Co-authored-by: Elastic Machine <elasticmachine@users.noreply.github.com>

commit_message: Removing invalid assertion from SnapshotLifecycleServiceTests (#107736)

issue_body:
While working on #107281 we noticed that when we were running `testPartialRestoreSnapshotThatIncludesDataStream` and we would include the global state, we would get the assertion error that the cluster state size does not match.

In this https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/107514 we introduce a test that demonstrates this issue without any other changes. Looking for other similar failures (#59140), we believe the reason is that there is something being mutated on the local node after the restoration. But this is just an assumption.

We are not sure if this is an issue in restore or in mapping, that's why we added both labels. Please feel free to change this accordingly.

**Build scan:**
https://gradle-enterprise.elastic.co/s/ceaa7hizih32e/tests/:modules:mapper-extras:internalClusterTest/org.elasticsearch.index.mapper.RestoreTemplateWithMatchOnlyTextMapperIT/test

commit_message:
Consider total number of aggregations not number of non-empty aggregations (#107536)

Test are failing because we are computing incorrectly the doc count error for terms aggregation. 
The only difference with the previous versions is that we are considering the number of empty aggregations
instead of the total number of aggregations when computing this value. Making this
change it makes the test happy.

commit_message:
Remove time-series ordinal rate tests in ESQL (#108148)

The rate aggregation will only be used in time-series aggregations, 
where ordinal grouping should never be applied. Therefore, we should
remove these tests to reduce unnecessary noise.

Closes #107568
Closes #107710

issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

8.9 and up

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

any

### Problem Description

Response headers such as `Warning: 299 Elasticsearch-8.* "Your license will expire in [6] days. Contact your administrator or update your license for continued use of features"` continue to be returned even though the license has been successfully [updated](https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/update-license.html).
Moreover, multiple such warnings might be returned for different countdowns.

In case, following a license update, no **new** warnings are returned (but the existing ones continue to show up), licensed functionality should not be impacted come the deadline.

### Steps to Reproduce

Authentication **and** auditing need to be enabled.
The license needs to expire 7 days from the current time.
Uploading a new license doesn't make the existing response warnings go away.
commit_message:
Fix lingering license warning header (#108031)

Fixes the `AcceptChannelHandler#accept` mucking with the netty worker thread context.

Fixes #107573

commit_message:
Unmute AzureStorageCleanupThirdPartyTests after credentials rotation (#107928)

Resolves: #107617, #107720, #107753

commit_message:
Re-enable threadpool blocking in Kibana system index test (#112569)

KibanaThreadPoolIT checks the Kibana system user can write (using the
system read/write threadpools) even when the normal read/write
threadpools are blocked. This commit re-enables a key part of the test
which was disabled.

closes #107625

issue_title: InternalStats can throw exceptions when serializing to XContent
commit_message:
Validate stats formatting in standard InternalStats constructor (#107678)

We want to validate stats formatting before we serialize to XContent, as chunked x-content serialization
 assumes that we don't throw exceptions at that point. It is not necessary to do it in the StreamInput constructor 
as this one has been serialise from an already checked object.

This commit adds starts formatting validation to the standard InternalStats constructor.

commit_message:
Remove time-series ordinal rate tests in ESQL (#108148)

The rate aggregation will only be used in time-series aggregations, 
where ordinal grouping should never be applied. Therefore, we should
remove these tests to reduce unnecessary noise.

Closes #107568
Closes #107710

issue_title: [ESQL] Difference in performance of two equivalent queries with st_centroid_agg
issue_body:
I have an index that contains a geo_point field. The following query takes 0.5 seconds to run:
```
from points | stats st_centroid_agg(location) 
```

But the following query takes more than 20 seconds:

```
from points | stats st_centroid_agg(TO_GEOPOINT(location)) 
```
Both queries are equivalent but the first one is reading values from doc values while the second one is reading them from _source. We can probably do better than that.
commit_message:
Rewrite away type converting functions that do not convert types (#108713)

* Rewrite away type converting functions that do not convert types

When a type converting function does not actually convert the type, it can be rewritten away. This opens the door for the resulting aliasing eval to also be rewritten into a project, which removes the ReferenceAttribute, and open the door for improved optimizations.

In particular it Fixes #107716

* Update docs/changelog/108713.yaml

* Optimization has tiny effect on results

Now the results match the other tests, where the optimization already worked.

* The optimization only affected backwards compatibility in one mixed-cluster test

* Update x-pack/plugin/esql/src/main/java/org/elasticsearch/xpack/esql/optimizer/LogicalPlanOptimizer.java

Co-authored-by: Alexander Spies <alexander.spies@elastic.co>

* Shorter class name

* Expand unit tests to cover a wider range of types

* Added extra mapping-extra.json to allow new tests to not conflict with others

---------

Co-authored-by: Alexander Spies <alexander.spies@elastic.co>

commit_message:
ES|QL: Fix warnings for number overflow in CSV SPEC tests  (#107745)

Fixes https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/issues/107733

Number overflow can return warnings with `InvalidArgumentException` or
`QlIllegalArgumentException` depending on the version of the node where
it's executed

issue_title: Annotated text fields should be stored by default when using index mode with synthetic source
issue_body:
### Description

Similar to `text` (#97039) if index mode uses synthetic source, it is correct to store text field because otherwise source can't be reconstructed and index creation fails.
commit_message:
Annotated text fields are stored by default with synthetic source (#107922)

This change follows existing implementation for text field.

Closes #107734.

issue_title: Block readiness on bad initial FileSettings
issue_body:
With https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch/pull/107738, we even report ready on bad initial file settings. This change was done to prevent existing clusters from never turning ready on restarts if cluster state contains erroneous file settings.

The behaviour should be fixed and integration tested as follows:
- If a new cluster fails to apply file settings for the very first time (due to the file being broken in some way), it should not turn ready.
- If, however, this happens for a cluster that already applied file settings previously, the cluster shall be considered ready regardless of the error.
commit_message:
Consider node ready on any file settings applied (#107738)

Readiness is blocked until file based settings having been applied. Yet in
the case that an error occurs in a subsequent file based settings
update, readiness will stop working. This was unintentional; if file
based settings have already been applied once, subsequent failures to
apply file based settings should not affect the running system.

This commit adjusts the readiness check to consider any file based
settings having been applied as allowing the node to be ready.

In a follow up (#107744) we are going to prevent readiness in case 
initial file settings of a new cluster cannot be applied.
---------

Co-authored-by: Moritz Mack <mmack@apache.org>

issue_title: ESQL: repetitive function calls could create a stackoverflow error
issue_body:
### Description

A query made simpler to write by `::` (but could probably be replicated with any nested function call) casting operator can lead to StackoverflowError:

`ROW v=123::string::version::string::version::......`

On my computer, ~1923 such casting calls leads to Stackoverflow. Interestingly enough, it's sometimes 1923, sometimes less if I call the query several times.
Also, still interesting, different stack traces where the overflow occurs:

1.

```
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.ql.tree.Node.transformChildren(Node.java:208)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.ql.tree.Node.transformDown(Node.java:179)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.ql.tree.Node.lambda$transformDown$8(Node.java:179)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.ql.tree.Node.transformChildren(Node.java:211)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.ql.tree.Node.transformDown(Node.java:179)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.ql.tree.Node.lambda$transformDown$8(Node.java:179)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.ql.tree.Node.transformChildren(Node.java:211)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.ql.tree.Node.transformDown(Node.java:179)
...
```

2.

```
        at java.base/java.lang.invoke.DirectMethodHandle.allocateInstance(DirectMethodHandle.java:501)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.evaluator.mapper.EvaluatorMapper.lambda$fold$1(EvaluatorMapper.java:48)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.expression.function.scalar.convert.AbstractConvertFunction.toEvaluator(AbstractConvertFunction.java:105)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.evaluator.mapper.EvaluatorMapper.fold(EvaluatorMapper.java:48)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.expression.function.scalar.EsqlScalarFunction.fold(EsqlScalarFunction.java:30)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.evaluator.mapper.EvaluatorMapper$1.eval(EvaluatorMapper.java:51)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.expression.function.scalar.convert.AbstractConvertFunction$AbstractEvaluator.eval(AbstractConvertFunction.java:138)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.evaluator.mapper.EvaluatorMapper.fold(EvaluatorMapper.java:62)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.expression.function.scalar.EsqlScalarFunction.fold(EsqlScalarFunction.java:30)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.evaluator.mapper.EvaluatorMapper$1.eval(EvaluatorMapper.java:51)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.expression.function.scalar.convert.AbstractConvertFunction$AbstractEvaluator.eval(AbstractConvertFunction.java:138)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.evaluator.mapper.EvaluatorMapper.fold(EvaluatorMapper.java:62)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.expression.function.scalar.EsqlScalarFunction.fold(EsqlScalarFunction.java:30)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.evaluator.mapper.EvaluatorMapper$1.eval(EvaluatorMapper.java:51)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.expression.function.scalar.convert.AbstractConvertFunction$AbstractEvaluator.eval(AbstractConvertFunction.java:138)
        at org.elasticsearch.xpack.esql.evaluator.mapper.EvaluatorMapper.fold(EvaluatorMapper.java:62)
```

Shorter queries are caught at parsing time, but it's not an ideal catch either, since the stackoverflow happens and the error is caught, an exception is thrown instead. The JVM could still be ok, but also not always.

```
                "type": "parsing_exception",
                "reason": "line -1:0: ESQL statement is too large, causing stack overflow when generating the parsing tree
```

commit_message:
[7.17] Fix Netty4TransportMultiPortIntegrationIT#testThatTransportClientCanConnect (#108112)

#103894 increased the range of available client ports on Windows for a node, but didn't update the range of ports that the transport client test iterates through in trying to connect to the node.

Both test failures reported in #107757 happened on Windows machines.

See #103894

Resolves #107757

commit_message:
[Test] Fix AsyncSearchResponse resource leak in security tests (#107809)

Closes #107759

commit_message:
Automatically adjust ignore_malformed only for the @timestamp (#109948)

We introduced automatic disabling ignore_malformed for the @timestamp field with #99346, but the change
was applied to any field with name @timestamp under any path, while it should have been applied
only to the top-level @timestamp field.

Relates to #107760

commit_message:
Fix test in `TimeSeriesDataStreamsIT` (#108177)

When ILM retries a step, it clears the `failed_step` field (until
it fails again). Therefore, it was possible for the test to see
`failed_step` equal to `null` everytime it polled. The field
`failed_step_retry_count` doesn't get cleared so it's more stable.

issue_title: `_field_caps` doesn't honour `ignore_unavailable` option with closed index
issue_body:
### Elasticsearch Version

8.14, also current master (@8201172)

### Installed Plugins

_No response_

### Java Version

_bundled_

### OS Version

macos

### Problem Description

Querying an inexistent index'es `_field_caps` with `ignore_unavailable=true` will work (returns no caps).
The same, but with an existing and closed index fails with `cluster_block_exception`.

### Steps to Reproduce

1. PUT myindex
2. POST myindex/_close
3. GET myindex/_field_caps?fields=*&ignore_unavailable=true

### Logs (if relevant)

Last step will fail with:
```
{
    "error": {
        "root_cause": [
            {
                "type": "cluster_block_exception",
                "reason": "index [myindex] blocked by: [FORBIDDEN/4/index closed];"
            }
        ],
        "type": "cluster_block_exception",
        "reason": "index [myindex] blocked by: [FORBIDDEN/4/index closed];"
    },
    "status": 403
}
```
commit_message:
Fields caps does not honour ignore_unavailable (#116021)

The IndicesOption has been updated into the FieldCapabilitiesRequest to encapsulate the following logic.

If we target a closed index and ignore_unavailable=false, we get an IndexClosedException, otherwise
 if the request contains ignore_unavailable=true, we safely skip the closed index.

commit_message:
Use direct method mapping for zstd (#108172)

JNA supports two types of mapping to native methods, proxying and direct
method mapping. Proxying is nicer for unit testing, but unfortunately
the proxied methods are lazily loaded. NativeAccess expects that methods
are linked during static init, before SecurityManager is initialized.
For any native methods called after security manager init, the proxied
method will fail.

This commit changes the zstd bindings to use direct method mapping so
that calling zstd methods does not fail when using JNA (pre Java 21).

closes #107504
closes #107770

commit_message:
[ML] Wait for all shards to be active when creating the ML stats index (#108202)

* Wait for all shards to be active when creating the ML stats index

* Unmute tests

* Wait for the stats index in cleanup

* more waiting for the stats index

* Add adminclient to ensureHealth

Co-authored-by: Pat Whelan <pat.whelan@elastic.co>

* fix errors causing build failures

---------

Co-authored-by: Max Hniebergall <max.hniebergall@elastic.co>
Co-authored-by: Elastic Machine <elasticmachine@users.noreply.github.com>

commit_message:
[ML] Wait for all shards to be active when creating the ML stats index (#108202)

* Wait for all shards to be active when creating the ML stats index

* Unmute tests

* Wait for the stats index in cleanup

* more waiting for the stats index

* Add adminclient to ensureHealth

Co-authored-by: Pat Whelan <pat.whelan@elastic.co>

* fix errors causing build failures

commit_message:
ESQL: Handle add near the end of time (#107968)

This updates our tests to handle the case where you are adding a
`TemporalAmount` to a date that is new `Long.MAX_VALUE`. It also adds a
test case that generates dates new that time just so we catch errors out
that way faster.

Closes #107817

commit_message:
Fix `testUpdatePolicyToAddPhasesYieldsInvalidActionsToBeSkipped` (#108128)

This test was failing due to the data stream being rolled over
multiple times, causing multiple searchable snapshots being
created, one of which got created _after_
`ESRestTestCase.wipeSearchableSnapshotsIndices()`, causing
a failure while trying to delete the snapshot repository.
